message:
feat(mux): Cx-aware cancellation checkpoints in DirectMuxClient

Add cooperative cancellation support to the mux client so that operations
bail out early when their Cx context is cancelled, rather than blocking
on socket I/O that will never produce a useful result.

Core change:
- Add checkpoint_mux_cx() helper that calls cx.checkpoint() and maps
  cancellation to DirectMuxError::Io with ErrorKind::Interrupted, including
  structured tracing with connection_id and phase for observability.

Wired into 4 transport boundaries:
- connect_with_cx: check before opening the Unix socket
- send_request_only_with_cx: check before serializing and before write_all
- read_next_pdu_with_cx: check before each blocking read loop iteration

This means a cancelled Cx will fail fast at:
1. Connection establishment (before socket open)
2. Request submission (before frame serialization)
3. Response reading (before blocking on socket data)

Test coverage (3 new tests, all gated on asupersync-runtime feature):
- send_request_only_with_precancelled_cx_fails_before_writing_frame:
  Verifies pre-cancelled Cx prevents any post-handshake frame from reaching
  the server, using a mock mux server that counts ListPanes requests.
- await_response_with_precancelled_cx_fails_before_reading_frame:
  Sends a real request, confirms the server receives it, then uses a
  pre-cancelled Cx for the response read — verifies cancellation without
  data loss (server sees exactly 1 request).
- connect_with_precancelled_cx_fails_before_opening_socket:
  Verifies pre-cancelled Cx prevents the TCP/Unix socket from being opened
  at all, using a listening server that asserts no connection was accepted.

Also adds test helpers cancelled_test_cx() and assert_cancelled_mux_io(),
and extends protocol_error_kind_treats_other_io_as_transient to cover
Interrupted alongside TimedOut.

Includes beads tracker update.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
